concepts.headline: The productivity data tells a more complex story.
concepts.subtext: Stanford tracked 16,000 workers over 9 months. The results challenge both extremes.
data_points: 73% | Workers who feel more productive at home
concepts.layout_description: Left-anchored hero pattern. Headline at 48px bold sits in upper-left quadrant with 72px left padding. A 2px warm sand accent bar runs above the headline. Below the headline, subtext at 16px in #555 with 32px gap. Stat tiles arranged in a 2x2 grid on the lower half: each tile is a square card on #f0efe9 surface with a 1px #e5e5e5 border, large DM Mono number at 40px in #111 and label below at 12px uppercase #888. Right side of canvas above the grid is reserved for a sparse particle dot network at 0.08 opacity. FLYWHEEL wordmark bottom-left at 12px uppercase DM Mono in #888. 72px padding on all edges.
concepts.color_usage: Background #ffffff. Headline #111. Subtext #555. Stat tiles on #f0efe9 with #e5e5e5 borders. Stat values in #111 DM Mono. Stat labels in #888. Accent bar #d7c8af. Particle dots #6b7a7c at 0.08 opacity.
concepts.reasoning: Four distinct quantitative proof points from the post make multi_stat_panel the natural fit. The data grid communicates scale and nuance simultaneously, and the left-anchored layout with a sparse right-side particle field preserves Flywheel's signature negative space.

concepts.headline: Intentional flexibility outperforms both extremes.
concepts.subtext: Stanford data: hybrid workers are 24% more productive than fully remote. The answer is in the middle.
concepts.layout_description: Centered statement pattern. Canvas is #faf9f6 warm off-white. A faint FLYWHEEL watermark at 160px bold uppercase sits centered at 0.03 opacity behind all content. Single text block centered vertically and horizontally: eyebrow label 'STANFORD STUDY, 16,000 WORKERS' in 11px DM Mono uppercase #888 with 0.07em letter-spacing sits above the headline. Headline at 56px weight 600 #111 with letter-spacing -0.04em, max-width 680px, centered. Subtext at 17px #555 centered below with 36px gap. A 2px warm sand horizontal accent bar 120px wide is centered directly below the subtext. FLYWHEEL wordmark bottom-center at 11px DM Mono uppercase #888. 72px padding all edges. Particle dots scattered in upper-right and lower-left corners at 0.07 opacity.
concepts.color_usage: Background #faf9f6. Headline #111. Eyebrow #888. Subtext #555. Accent bar #d7c8af. FLYWHEEL watermark #000 at 0.03 opacity. Particle dots #6b7a7c at 0.07 opacity.
concepts.reasoning: The post's core argument is a single declarative insight: neither extreme works, flexibility wins. A centered bold statement card distills that to its essence. The FLYWHEEL watermark and minimal particle texture preserve editorial gravity without adding noise.

concepts.headline: Hybrid workers are 24% more productive than remote.
concepts.subtext: Stanford tracked 16,000 workers for 9 months. The sweet spot is intentional flexibility, not a mandate.
data_points: Hybrid productivity advantage over fully remote workers, Stanford University
concepts.layout_description: Left-anchored hero pattern. Upper-left: eyebrow label 'STANFORD UNIVERSITY, 9-MONTH STUDY' in 11px DM Mono uppercase #888. Below eyebrow with 24px gap: headline at 52px weight 600 #111 letter-spacing -0.04em, max-width 600px. Below headline with 28px gap: subtext at 17px #555, max-width 520px. Hero stat block sits mid-canvas left: '24%' in 96px DM Mono weight 400 #111 with a 2px warm sand left border and 20px left padding. A short 1px #e5e5e5 horizontal rule separates the stat from a secondary note '13% remote gain vs. office' in 13px DM Mono #888 below it. Right half of canvas: particle dot network at 0.10 opacity. FLYWHEEL wordmark bottom-left 11px DM Mono uppercase #888. 72px edge padding throughout.
concepts.color_usage: Background #ffffff. Headline #111. Subtext #555. Eyebrow #888. Hero stat #111 DM Mono. Stat left border #d7c8af. Secondary stat label #888. Rule #e5e5e5. Particle dots #6b7a7c at 0.10 opacity.
concepts.reasoning: The 24% hybrid productivity advantage is the sharpest, most counterintuitive data point in the post. A stat callout isolates it as the hero number, giving the reader an immediate anchor before the supporting context elaborates.

selection_reasoning: The multi_stat_panel is the strongest choice because the post is fundamentally data-driven, presenting four distinct numbers that together build an argument. Showing all four stats in a structured grid lets the viewer grasp the full evidential picture at a glance, which is more intellectually honest and visually compelling than isolating one number or reducing everything to a declaration. The 2x2 tile grid with DM Mono numerals against warm off-white surfaces exemplifies Flywheel's structured data layout pattern, and the left-anchored headline above the grid preserves the required asymmetric balance and negative space.
